title: Surrogate Decision Authority Changes Before Execution
scenario: A surrogate decision-maker authorizes care, but their authority changes before the clinical action executes.
initial_state: Consent was authorized by a surrogate decision-maker recognized at the time of authorization.
state_transition: The surrogate's decision authority is superseded before execution.
execution_boundary: The clinical action is evaluated before execution against the current consent authority state.
constitutional_evaluation: Consent continuity requires that the authority supporting consent remain valid at execution time.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because the consent authority that supported authorization is no longer valid.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when surrogate consent authority changes before execution.
clinical_action: Execute care plan authorized by prior surrogate
